response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong breadth across web, mobile, and embedded systems, but the resume shows minimal hands-on experience with the core stack required for this role. Java appears only as a listed skill with brief mentions at Altom and National Instruments (a decade ago), Kotlin is absent, and there is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or IDE-internals work. Recent experience is centered on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which do not align with an enterprise JVM tooling position.
strengths: Long track record building complex systems across multiple languages and domains
Prior IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI (C/C++ IDE components)
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, workshops, open-source tooling)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only superficial Java exposure
No evidence of Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or enterprise JVM ecosystem work
Recent roles focus on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not JVM or language tooling
